facts.fact: htmx lets you access AJAX, CSS Transitions, WebSockets, and Server Sent Events directly in HTML using attributes.
htmx is small (~16k min.gz'd) and dependency-free.
htmx v4 is in beta as of the 2026-06-04 scrape, with a target release date of Summer '26 (more details at four.htmx.org).

Core htmx attributes include hx-get, hx-post, hx-swap, hx-target, and hx-trigger, where hx-swap controls how content swaps in (e.g., outerHTML, beforeend, afterend) and hx-trigger specifies the event that triggers the request.
htmx supports issuing GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE HTTP requests via hx-get, hx-post, hx-put, hx-patch, and hx-delete attributes.
htmx is the successor to intercooler.js.
htmx 2.x dropped Internet Explorer support; the 1.x code-line (v1.htmx.org) retains IE support and is supported in perpetuity.
htmx supports out-of-band swaps via hx-swap-oob and hx-select-oob, allowing content to be swapped somewhere other than the target.
The quick-start example uses htmx version 2.0.10 loaded from a jsDelivr CDN script tag.
htmx defines CSS classes including htmx-request (applied during an ongoing request), htmx-indicator, htmx-swapping, htmx-settling, and htmx-added.

htmx sends request headers such as HX-Request (always 'true'), HX-Trigger, HX-Target, HX-Current-URL, and HX-Boosted; and supports response headers like HX-Redirect, HX-Location, HX-Push-Url, HX-Retarget, HX-Reswap, and HX-Trigger.

htmx fires a lifecycle of events around requests/swaps such as htmx:beforeRequest, htmx:configRequest, htmx:beforeSwap, htmx:afterSwap, and htmx:afterSettle, which can be used to customize or cancel behavior.
An accompanying book, Hypermedia Systems (hypermedia.systems), teaches building Hypermedia-Driven Applications with htmx.
htmx claims to have reduced code base sizes by 67% compared with React in a real-world port case study.
